political.s: Popeyes operates as a wholly owned subsidiary of Restaurant Brands International (RBI, NYSE: QSR) and does not maintain its own PAC. Prior to the 2017 acquisition, Popeyes' then-chairman John M. Cranor III made personal donations to Democratic politicians in Florida, but no corporate PAC was ever established for the brand. Political activity relevant to Popeyes is now channeled through parent company RBI's broader lobbying and government affairs operation.
political.amt: Not disclosed (no standalone Popeyes PAC on record)

charity.s: The Popeyes Foundation, a 501(c)(3) established in 2018, operates the 'Serving with Love' initiative focused on hunger relief and employee hardship assistance. The Foundation dispersed $475,000 to 75 nonprofits through its Food Love Grants program, supporting over 1.4 million meals, and directed approximately $202,000 to 97 team members facing catastrophic hardship. RBI's 2023 sustainability report also notes the Foundation supported 65+ local nonprofits providing food assistance.
charity.amt: ~$675,000+ annually (Foundation grants and team member support combined)
charity.focus: Hunger relief, food access, employee emergency assistance

environment.s: Popeyes falls under RBI's 'Restaurant Brands for Good' sustainability framework, which has been included in the Dow Jones Sustainability North America Index for three consecutive years. Commitments include 100% cage-free eggs by 2025, elimination of EPS foam, RSPO-certified sustainable palm oil, and antibiotic-free chicken supply chains in the US and Canada. However, the World Benchmarking Alliance rated RBI as only average in its 2023 Food and Agriculture Benchmark, noting gaps in scope 1/2/3 emissions tracking and plastic/packaging waste quantification.
sources: RBI Restaurant Brands for Good Report 2024

labor.s: Popeyes franchisees have faced repeated and serious labor violations. In 2024, a California franchisee paid $212,000 after DOL investigations found child labor violations at three locations—the third such citation for that operator. Massachusetts AG cited Popeyes franchise operators for child labor and earned sick time violations, resulting in $212,516 in penalties. A Tampa Popeyes worker also won an NLRB unfair labor practice case after being fired for striking, resulting in required changes to labor rules across 100+ stores and reinstatement.

dei.s: Popeyes has a Glassdoor DEI rating of 3.6 out of 5, equal to the sector average for restaurants and food service. RBI's parent-level sustainability reports disclose women's representation in its workforce and board, targeting at least 30% female directors. Popeyes itself does not appear in the HRC Corporate Equality Index as a standalone participant, and no specific LGBTQ+ workplace policy disclosures are publicly attributed to the Popeyes brand independently.

animals.s: Popeyes signed the Better Chicken Commitment in 2020, pledging improved living conditions, higher-welfare bird strains, and an end to live-shackle slaughter, to be implemented by 2024. The brand also committed to 100% cage-free eggs in North America, Western Europe, and Latin America by end of 2025 and has eliminated antibiotics important to human medicine from its US/Canada chicken supply chain. PETA and Compassion in World Farming have previously criticized Popeyes for slow implementation, and the brand's core business involves large-scale animal slaughter.

guns.s: Popeyes is a quick-service restaurant chain and does not sell or manufacture firearms. The brand has no known corporate policy specifically addressing guns in its restaurants beyond general franchise operational guidelines.

privacy.s: Common Sense Privacy evaluation flagged Popeyes with a 'Warning' rating, noting that personal information is sold or rented to third parties, shared for third-party marketing, and used to track and target advertisements across other platforms. Popeyes' own privacy policy acknowledges collecting personal data under CCPA definitions and permits disclosure to third parties in multiple circumstances including business transfers and fraud prevention. This practice of data monetization and ad targeting places Popeyes below recommended privacy standards.

execPay.s: As Popeyes' parent, RBI's CEO earned approximately $29 million in 2023, a ratio of roughly 919:1 compared to the median RBI employee who earned $31,525 annually. This is one of the highest CEO-to-worker pay ratios in the restaurant industry, reflecting a heavily franchised model where median corporate pay is elevated but still dramatically outpaced by executive compensation. Popeyes does not separately disclose its own executive pay ratio as a subsidiary.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
